outputAuthor.text: John R Williams1, Pam Mantri2 and John Thomas2

outputAbstract.text: Abstract. Traditional top-down organizational designs often suffer from inefficiencies, rigidity, and poor adaptability in the face of rapid technological change and global data flows. Organizations today must simultaneously manage fast-cycle changes while optimizing established business processes. This paper proposes a novel framework that integrates Axiomatic Design (AD) and Complex Adaptive Systems (CAS) to create dynamic, efficient, and data-driven organizational structures. By leveraging data, process, and organizational mining, organizations can proactively identify inefficiencies, optimize decision-making, and restructure business models. The AD–CAS framework offers a principled methodology for building resilient, adaptive, and analytically driven organizations. It enables self-optimization, real-time data insights, and structural flexibility in increasingly complex environments.
